title: Subscriptions (Recurring Billing)
lede: Turn one-time customers into predictable, recurring revenue. Subscriptions let you bill any service on autopilot — weekly, monthly, annual, or any custom cadence — with automatic invoices, saved-card charging, trials, pauses, and refund-aware cancellations. This guide takes you from an empty list to a live, self-billing subscription.

steps.title: Open the Subscriptions list
steps.content:
Everything recurring lives on one screen — every plan, its status, and how much monthly revenue it represents.

  1. In the top navigation, click CRM, then click the Subscriptions tab (or go directly to /pipeline/subscriptions).
  2. The table shows one row per subscription with these columns:
    • Subscription Name — click it to open the full detail page.
    • Number — the subscription's reference number.
    • Status — a colored badge: Draft, Due, Trial, Active, Paused, Past Due, Cancelled, or Expired. An orange Cancelling pill appears next to Active when the customer is scheduled to cancel at the end of the current period.
    • Account — the company the subscription is linked to.
    • Billing Cycle — Weekly, Monthly, Quarterly, Semi-Annual, Annual, or Biennial.
    • Billing Amount — what the customer pays each cycle.
    • MRR and ARR — the subscription's Monthly and Annual Recurring Revenue, calculated automatically from the amount and cycle. Click either column header to sort.
    • Start Date and Next Billing — when it began and when the next charge lands.
    • Auto-Renew — Yes (green) keeps billing until cancelled; use the column's filter to show only Auto-Renew or Manual rows.
    • Actions — View (eye), Edit (pencil), and Delete (trash) icons.
💡 Phone-number subscriptions (the ones iMash creates when you buy a number) are managed from the Phone Numbers page — their trash icon redirects you there instead of deleting.

images.alt: Subscriptions list showing draft and active subscriptions with status, billing cycle and actions columns
images.caption: The Subscriptions list — every recurring plan with its status, cycle, MRR/ARR, and per-row actions

images.alt: Subscriptions list showing draft and active subscriptions with status, billing cycle and actions columns
images.caption: The Subscriptions list — every recurring plan with its status, cycle, MRR/ARR, and per-row actions

steps.title: Search and filter the list
steps.content:
Click Show Filters in the top-right to reveal the filter bar:

  • Search subscriptions… — type part of a name and press Enter.
  • Filter by status — narrow to a single status: Draft, Due, Trial, Active, Paused, Past Due, Cancelled, or Expired.
  • Show: — the view selector, your quickest lens on the list:
    • Active only (default) — only subscriptions currently billing.
    • Cancelling — active subscriptions scheduled to end at period end.
    • All live — everything that isn't cancelled or deleted (includes drafts, trials, paused, and past-due).
    • Cancelled / Expired — plans that have ended.
    • Deleted only — soft-deleted rows, marked with a red Deleted pill.
    • Everything — the whole history, no filter.
  • Start date / End date — show only subscriptions that started within a date range.
  • Filter by owner — see one team member's subscriptions.
💡 Just created a draft and can't see it? That's the default Show: Active only view hiding it — switch to All live and your draft appears.

images.alt: Filter bar with search box, status filter, view selector, date range and owner filter
images.caption: The filter bar — search, status, the Show view selector, date range, and owner

images.alt: Filter bar with search box, status filter, view selector, date range and owner filter
images.caption: The filter bar — search, status, the Show view selector, date range, and owner

steps.title: Create a subscription — the basics
steps.content:
Time to build your first recurring plan. Click Create Subscription in the top-right of the list — the New Subscription form opens.

  1. Subscription Name (required) — a name the customer would recognize, e.g. Monthly Lawn Care — Riverside Apartments.
  2. Billing Cycle — pick Weekly, Monthly, Quarterly, Semi-annual, Annual, Biennial, or Custom…. Choosing Custom reveals Every and Unit fields so you can bill every N day(s), week(s), month(s), or year(s) — every 45 days, every 2 weeks, anything.
  3. Currency — USD, EUR, GBP, CAD, or AUD.
  4. Auto-Renew — leave Yes so the plan keeps billing each cycle until cancelled.
  5. Link the customer with the Account, Contact, or Lead picker — start typing a name and select the match. You only need one of the three; if you set several, the account wins, then the contact, then the lead.
  6. Start Date — when billing begins. Leave it empty to start today.

images.alt: New Subscription form with name, billing cycle, currency, auto-renew, customer pickers, saved card, trial and cancellation policy fields
images.caption: The New Subscription form — cycle, currency, customer, payment, trial, and policy in one screen

images.alt: New Subscription form with name, billing cycle, currency, auto-renew, customer pickers, saved card, trial and cancellation policy fields
images.caption: The New Subscription form — cycle, currency, customer, payment, trial, and policy in one screen

steps.title: Add line items — what the customer pays each cycle
steps.content:
Scroll to the Line items card at the bottom of the form. Each line is a product or service that bills every cycle, and the blue banner keeps a running Total per cycle as you type.

  1. In the first row, enter the Item name (e.g. Weekly lawn mowing service).
  2. Set Qty and Unit price. Optional Discount and Tax amounts adjust the Line total.
  3. Click Add line (top-right of the card) for each additional item; remove a row with its trash icon (at least one line must remain).
⚠️ At least one line item with a name is required — the sum of all line totals becomes the subscription's Billing Amount.

images.alt: Line items editor with two service lines, quantities, unit prices and the per-cycle total banner
images.caption: Line items — each row bills every cycle; the banner shows the total per cycle

images.alt: Line items editor with two service lines, quantities, unit prices and the per-cycle total banner
images.caption: Line items — each row bills every cycle; the banner shows the total per cycle

steps.title: Choose how the customer pays — plus trials, end dates, and refund policy
steps.content:
These four settings decide how money actually moves:

  • Saved card on file (optional) — enabled once you've picked a customer. Two modes:
    • Pick a saved card — renewals are charged to that card automatically, no customer action needed.
    • Leave it empty — the customer receives the first invoice's payment link and pays with their own card. The card they enter is saved and used for future renewals automatically.
  • Free trial? — switch to Yes and pick a Trial end date. The customer isn't charged until the trial ends, and the subscription shows the Trial status until then. Leave it at No and billing starts immediately.
  • Has end date? — switch to Yes and pick a date to stop the subscription automatically; leave No and it continues until cancelled.
  • Cancellation policy — what happens if you ever cancel immediately:
    • No refund (default) — just stop the next billing cycle.
    • SaaS model — refund the unused portion of the current period.
    • Refund the last billing cycle in full.
    The policy only applies to immediate cancellations — cancelling at period end never refunds, because the customer used the full period.
  • Tags — optional labels for organizing and filtering.

Now click Save Draft in the top-right. Nothing is charged yet — the subscription is saved as a Draft and you land on its detail page.

💡 Recurring charging runs on your connected Stripe account — connect it under Settings → Payments before activating your first subscription.

images.alt: Saved card on file field with explanation of auto-charge versus invoice link
images.caption: Saved card on file — pick a card to auto-charge, or leave empty to send an invoice link

images.alt: Saved card on file field with explanation of auto-charge versus invoice link
images.caption: Saved card on file — pick a card to auto-charge, or leave empty to send an invoice link

steps.title: Read the detail page — your subscription command center
steps.content:
Click any subscription's name to open its detail page. The Overview tab shows:

  • Four stat tiles — Per-cycle total, MRR (monthly recurring revenue), ARR (annual recurring revenue), and Lifetime Value (everything this customer has paid so far).
  • A status banner — a blue Draft — not activated notice while nothing has been charged, or a green Subscription is active banner once recurring billing is running (with an orange Will cancel at period end tag if a cancellation is scheduled).
  • The details table — name, number, status, billing cycle, auto-renew, the linked Account / Contact / Lead (clickable), start date, trial end date, Next Billing Date (with a day countdown), end date, cancellation policy, and any tags.
  • Line items — the items billed each cycle with quantity, unit price, discount, tax, and totals.

images.alt: Subscription detail page with stat tiles, draft banner and details table
images.caption: The detail page — stat tiles, status banner, and the full details table

images.alt: Subscription detail page with stat tiles, draft banner and details table
images.caption: The detail page — stat tiles, status banner, and the full details table

images.alt: Line items card on the subscription detail page
images.caption: Line items on the detail page — what bills every cycle

images.alt: Line items card on the subscription detail page
images.caption: Line items on the detail page — what bills every cycle

steps.title: Activate the subscription — two ways to start billing
steps.content:
This is the moment your draft becomes real revenue. A draft's header shows two activation buttons:

  1. Create & Send Invoice — sets up the recurring billing and generates the first invoice without charging anything. The subscription moves to Due; share the invoice's payment link with the customer, and once they pay, the card they used is saved and future renewals charge automatically. Use this when you don't have the customer's card yet.
  2. Activate & Charge — creates the subscription and charges the saved card immediately. On success the status jumps straight to Active. This button is only enabled when a Saved card on file is picked on the subscription (click Edit to pick one).

Also in the header: Edit reopens the form, and Delete removes the subscription.

💡 After activating, give the page a few seconds — the first invoice is generated asynchronously and appears on the Invoices tab shortly after.

images.alt: Draft subscription header with Create and Send Invoice, Activate and Charge, Edit and Delete buttons
images.caption: A draft's header — Create & Send Invoice (no charge yet) vs Activate & Charge (needs a saved card)

images.alt: Draft subscription header with Create and Send Invoice, Activate and Charge, Edit and Delete buttons
images.caption: A draft's header — Create & Send Invoice (no charge yet) vs Activate & Charge (needs a saved card)

steps.title: Track generated invoices
steps.content:
Every billing cycle produces a real invoice — and they all collect on the Invoices tab of the detail page (the tab label shows the count).

  1. Click the Invoices tab.
  2. Each row shows the invoice Number, Date, Status, Payment status (paid green, failed red), Total, Paid, and Due amounts.
  3. Click Open on any row to jump to the full invoice — from there you can download the PDF, resend the payment link, or refund.

If a renewal charge fails, the subscription flips to Past Due so you can spot it from the list and chase the payment.

images.alt: Invoices tab of a subscription showing the invoice table columns
images.caption: The Invoices tab — one invoice per billing cycle, each openable in full

images.alt: Invoices tab of a subscription showing the invoice table columns
images.caption: The Invoices tab — one invoice per billing cycle, each openable in full

steps.title: Pause, resume, or cancel
steps.content:
Life happens — customers travel, budgets change. The detail-page header adapts to the subscription's status:

  • Pause — available while the plan is Active, Due, Trial, or Past Due. Billing stops but the subscription is kept; the status becomes Paused.
  • Resume — appears on a paused subscription and restarts billing.
  • Cancel — appears once the subscription is activated. It opens the Cancel subscription dialog:
    1. By default the cancellation happens at the end of the current period — the customer keeps access until then, no more invoices after, and no refund (they used what they paid for). The list shows an orange Cancelling pill until the period ends.
    2. Tick Cancel immediately (skip end-of-period grace) to end it right now. The dialog's banner tells you exactly what your cancellation policy will do: no refund, an automatic prorated refund of the unused time (SaaS policy), or a full refund of the last cycle.
    3. Optionally pick a Cancellation reason (internal) — it's stored on the subscription for your reporting, never shown to the customer.
    4. Click Confirm cancellation.
⚠️ Policy-driven refunds fire only on immediate cancellations. If an automatic refund fails, you'll see a warning — issue it manually from the invoice.

images.alt: Action buttons on an active subscription showing Pause, Edit and Delete
images.caption: An active subscription's actions — Pause appears while the plan is billing

images.alt: Action buttons on an active subscription showing Pause, Edit and Delete
images.caption: An active subscription's actions — Pause appears while the plan is billing

steps.title: Delete a subscription (and when not to)
steps.content:
Deleting is the nuclear option — prefer Cancel for anything a customer once paid for, so the history stays intact.

  1. Click the trash icon on a list row (or Delete on the detail page).
  2. Read the confirmation carefully: if the subscription is currently billing, deleting cancels it immediately — the customer loses access right away — and then removes it from the CRM.
  3. Click Delete to confirm.

Deleted rows aren't gone forever on screen: switch the list's view selector to Show: Deleted only to review them, marked with a red Deleted pill.

⚠️ Phone-number subscriptions can't be deleted here — their trash icon takes you to the Phone Numbers page, where releasing the number handles the billing correctly.

images.alt: Delete subscription confirmation popover with Cancel and Delete buttons
images.caption: The delete confirmation — active subscriptions are cancelled immediately before removal

images.alt: Delete subscription confirmation popover with Cancel and Delete buttons
images.caption: The delete confirmation — active subscriptions are cancelled immediately before removal

support.content:
If you run into any issues or have questions:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
  • 💬 Join our Discord community
